jokes.text: I love you, too, Tom. Go fuck yourself.
jokes.setup: Sweet exchange of 'I love you' between Selina and Tom
jokes.punchline: Selina immediately follows with 'Go fuck yourself'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina - combines affection with vulgarity in rapid succession. Economical and character-specific.

jokes.text: Because of my court-ordered chemical castration.
jokes.setup: Someone explaining why they were hired
jokes.punchline: Chemical castration as a job qualification
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Richard moment - delivers shocking information with his characteristic obliviousness.

jokes.text: Is chocolate bad for dogs? Oh no, not bad. Deadly.
jokes.setup: Innocent question about chocolate and dogs
jokes.punchline: Casual escalation from 'not bad' to 'deadly'
jokes.explanation: Nice structure with the correction escalating the danger rather than minimizing it.

jokes.text: It sounds like Dr. Seuss fucked Maya Angelou in the yuzz-ma-tuzz and then filled her all up with snoozily-scuzz.
jokes.setup: Commenting on Kemi's protest chant style
jokes.punchline: Elaborate sexual metaphor using made-up Dr. Seuss language
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly creative combination of high and low culture with invented Seussian sexual terminology. Memorable and quotable.

jokes.text: Not even really showing. You look beautiful.
jokes.setup: Amy's pregnancy situation
jokes.punchline: Gary's inappropriate comment about Amy not showing while she's seeking an abortion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary moment - well-intentioned but completely tone-deaf to the situation.

jokes.text: Without the 'four years ago.' OK. Iowa! What do we want?
jokes.setup: Trying to get crowd to chant properly
jokes.punchline: Crowd keeps adding the part she doesn't want, she tries to redirect
jokes.explanation: Classic political rally cringe - the candidate losing control of their own messaging.

jokes.text: When I say 'shit,' you say 'show.'
jokes.setup: Rally is going poorly, Ben trying to help
jokes.punchline: Ben's completely inappropriate rally chant suggestion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben - trying to be helpful but suggesting something completely unhinged for a political rally.

jokes.text: Are angels real?
jokes.setup: Middle of political crisis discussion
jokes.punchline: Completely random theological question
jokes.explanation: Perfect non-sequitur that captures the chaos of the campaign environment.

jokes.text: Yeah, well, somebody sneezed on our campaign, now we're bleeding out our assholes.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Kemi Fever spreading
jokes.punchline: Extended medical metaphor ending in graphic imagery
jokes.explanation: Vivid and disgusting metaphor that perfectly captures their campaign's dire situation.

jokes.text: Last thing I need is my picture being taken eating dick-shaped food. I'd rather eat a food-shaped dick.
jokes.setup: Discussing eating corn dogs at the fair
jokes.punchline: Selina's preference reversal with sexual imagery
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that shows Selina's quick wit and complete lack of filter. The reversal is unexpected and memorable.

jokes.text: Maybe somebody should be interviewing the 'Times' about why they write so much about modern dance.
jokes.setup: NY Times interviewing Keith Quinn
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical criticism of Times' arts coverage
jokes.explanation: Random deflection that shows the team's desperation to criticize anything related to their problems.

jokes.text: How are we going to RU486 Kemi's campaign?
jokes.setup: Discussing how to stop Kemi
jokes.punchline: Using abortion pill as a verb for destroying campaigns
jokes.explanation: Darkly clever wordplay that's both topically relevant and shockingly inappropriate. Very Veep.

jokes.text: No offense, Amy.
jokes.setup: After abortion reference
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging Amy's upcoming abortion
jokes.explanation: Good callback acknowledging the personal connection to the political metaphor.

jokes.text: My God, pick a lane. Jesus.
jokes.setup: Catherine expressing attraction to strong women like Kemi
jokes.punchline: Selina's exasperated response to her daughter's sexuality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina - more annoyed by indecision than by her daughter's sexuality.

jokes.text: A good union or, like, teachers?
jokes.setup: Dan reporting Kemi picked up a union
jokes.punchline: Selina's dismissive attitude toward teachers' unions
jokes.explanation: Shows Selina's political calculations and casual dismissal of education workers.

jokes.text: Asbestos was the name of their pit bull. It was a rescue that killed its first owner.
jokes.setup: Richard's uncle died from asbestos
jokes.punchline: Asbestos was actually a murderous dog
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - starts as sympathetic story, becomes absurdist Richard tale about a killer dog. Perfect structure and timing.

jokes.text: That's why you have to go to breeders.
jokes.setup: Story about rescue dog that killed its owner
jokes.punchline: Practical advice delivered deadpan
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response that treats the absurd dog story as requiring practical pet advice.

jokes.text: Fantastic. Her Achilles cock.
jokes.setup: Realizing Andrew is Selina's weakness
jokes.punchline: Ben's malapropism mixing 'Achilles heel' with crude imagery
jokes.explanation: Classic Ben malapropism that accidentally creates sexual imagery while trying to sound smart.

jokes.text: I'm drinking Odwalla like some country lesbian who just got to the big city.
jokes.setup: Complaining about lack of good green juice in Iowa
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific demographic comparison
jokes.explanation: Hilariously specific cultural stereotype delivered as casual complaint about beverage options.

jokes.text: Happy Abortion Eve.
jokes.setup: Richard arriving at office
jokes.punchline: Treating Amy's abortion like a holiday
jokes.explanation: Perfectly inappropriate Richard moment - his oblivious cheerfulness about a sensitive situation.

jokes.text: OK, I don't know if I should be sad or turned on.
jokes.setup: Everyone knows about Amy's abortion
jokes.punchline: Ben's inappropriate sexual confusion about the situation
jokes.explanation: Typical Ben inappropriate response, though not as sharp as his best moments.

jokes.text: It's my mother's side of the family. Speltt.
jokes.setup: Explaining the Speltt family motto
jokes.punchline: Richard has been saying his own family name wrong
jokes.explanation: Perfect Richard absurdity - not even knowing how to pronounce his own family name correctly.

jokes.text: Then why am I not in prison for punching the president?
jokes.setup: Jonah practicing debate about being tough on crime
jokes.punchline: Admitting to assaulting the president as evidence against crime fighting
jokes.explanation: Classic Jonah logic - accidentally confessing to crimes while trying to sound tough.

jokes.text: I'm Jonah Ryan, and I wanna suck this message's hot clam.
jokes.setup: Jonah doing campaign ad
jokes.punchline: Completely inappropriate sexual ending to political message
jokes.explanation: Pure Jonah chaos - completely destroying a campaign ad with bizarre sexual language.

jokes.text: He did a really funny Chinese voice. Yeah, I expressed how horny I was.
jokes.setup: Discussing Jonah's wedding vows improv
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of racist and inappropriate wedding content
jokes.explanation: Escalating awfulness - each detail makes the wedding vows worse.

jokes.text: Well, I don't see vagina color.
jokes.setup: Focus group uncomfortable with white man kicking black woman
jokes.punchline: Jonah's attempt at colorblind rhetoric applied to genitalia
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Jonah logic - applying racial colorblindness to anatomy in the worst possible context.

jokes.text: I thought we both understood I was lying.
jokes.setup: Selina confronting Andrew about fund illegality
jokes.punchline: Andrew's matter-of-fact admission about mutual deception
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andrew - casually admitting to mutual criminality as if it was understood social protocol.

jokes.text: Can you fuck off? Can do, skipper.
jokes.setup: Someone asking for Selina's time
jokes.punchline: Cheerful compliance with being told to fuck off
jokes.explanation: Nice contrast between Selina's hostility and the staffer's unflappable politeness.

jokes.text: Don't worry, Lee. You won't get pregnant. I'll pull out and cum on your back. Again, I thought we both understood I was lying.
jokes.setup: Andrew promising to keep Lee out of trouble
jokes.punchline: Lee's sexual metaphor followed by Andrew's recurring lie excuse
jokes.explanation: Effective callback that reinforces Andrew's pattern while adding crude sexual parallel.

jokes.text: He was lucky not to get dumped out of a pickup truck into a quarry.
jokes.setup: Discussing using Ford's funeral as template
jokes.punchline: Selina's dismissive view of Ford's worthiness
jokes.explanation: Dark and specific imagery that shows Selina's contempt for Gerald Ford in visceral terms.

jokes.text: More of a sort of a Princess Diana thing. But classy.
jokes.setup: Describing her funeral vision
jokes.punchline: Calling Princess Diana's funeral not classy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina arrogance - implying she could improve on one of history's most watched funerals.

jokes.text: It is literally the definition of retarded.
jokes.setup: Jonah complaining about undercard debate placement
jokes.punchline: Using slur immediately after sensitivity training
jokes.explanation: Shows Jonah's complete inability to learn or filter himself, even in public.

jokes.text: Why, is he retarded? No, but you might be.
jokes.setup: Teddy warning about using 'retarded' in front of reporter
jokes.punchline: Jonah's oblivious response and Teddy's retort
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Jonah's complete obliviousness and Teddy's exasperation.

jokes.text: No. My pediatrician ran the tests. Besides, if I was, you'd know. I'd have retard face or whatever.
jokes.setup: Defending against suggestion he might be intellectually disabled
jokes.punchline: Jonah's offensive and ignorant explanation
jokes.explanation: Shows Jonah's ignorance but relies heavily on offensive language for shock value.

jokes.text: It's 'Fat Guy.' Hey, Mike, have you given any thought to that thing that we talked about?
jokes.setup: Mike thinks he's called 'Hat Guy'
jokes.punchline: Wendy's casual correction while transitioning to serious topic
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Mike's delusions about his nickname.

jokes.text: That's a busy beaver. Yes. Yes, indeed.
jokes.setup: Mother with many children at fair
jokes.punchline: Selina's inappropriate sexual innuendo about motherhood
jokes.explanation: Typical Selina inappropriate comment, though mother's oblivious agreement helps.

jokes.text: The current version only asks for donations and, for some reason, deletes your address book. Technically, it's more of a virus.
jokes.setup: Asked if they have a campaign app
jokes.punchline: Kent's matter-of-fact description of their terrible app
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kent deadpan - clinical description of technological incompetence.

jokes.text: How the fuck did that happen? It's like 'Flowers for Algernon.' I understand that reference now. It's a book.
jokes.setup: Mike asking an actual coherent question
jokes.punchline: Mike's proud literary reference and explanation
jokes.explanation: Great Mike moment - both his brief intelligence and need to explain basic literary knowledge.

jokes.text: Um... mentally retarded? No, that-- that's wrong. I see where your head is at, though, and you are technically correct.
jokes.setup: Sensitivity trainer asking for alternative to 'retarded'
jokes.punchline: Jonah's worse alternative and trainer's professional struggle
jokes.explanation: Shows both Jonah's inability to learn and the trainer's professional struggle to remain positive.

jokes.text: That's so gay. Oh, guys, come on. I didn't mean like gay gay or like 'This guy and his husband are gay.' I meant gay like, uh, mentally retarded.
jokes.setup: Jonah responding to empathy training
jokes.punchline: Trying to clarify one slur by using another
jokes.explanation: Pure Jonah logic failure - making things worse while trying to explain. Gets points for character consistency but relies on offensive language.

jokes.text: She doesn't call that rape, by the way, that's just regular sex to her.
jokes.setup: Dan accused of rape-adjacent behavior
jokes.punchline: Sophie's casual description of her sister's sexual dysfunction
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that reveals family dysfunction, though edges toward exploitative territory.

jokes.text: Where is that Fatty McFatty hiding today? She aborted her unborn fetus.
jokes.setup: Selina asking about Amy's location
jokes.punchline: Richard's clinical description delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast between Selina's juvenile insult and Richard's overly clinical response.

jokes.text: I was an agricultural advisor. Nothing more.
jokes.setup: Someone calling Dan a Green Beret based on his aggressive behavior
jokes.punchline: Dan's modest denial of military service
jokes.explanation: Nice understated response that contrasts with Dan's aggressive protective behavior.

jokes.text: Well, I did think about this. I considered it, and I cried, and yeah, suck my cock, I even prayed a little, and here I am.
jokes.setup: Anti-abortion protesters telling her to think of children
jokes.punchline: Amy's emotional journey ending with vulgar defiance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Amy - vulnerable honesty mixed with aggressive defiance. The prayer admission adds depth.

jokes.text: So you can back the fuck off, you hypocritical cunts, before I show up to the piss puddle that is your house and protest your husband whacking it to your daughter's seventh grade yearbook.
jokes.setup: Amy already angry at protesters
jokes.punchline: Graphic threat involving specific sexual imagery
jokes.explanation: Amy at her most vicious - creative insults but borders on gratuitous shock value.

jokes.text: Her records were sealed. She was 16. She accidentally hit him with her Volkswagen Cabriolet. She is an actual murderer?! Yes! Who drives an import!
jokes.setup: Learning about Kemi's car accident
jokes.punchline: Selina more outraged by foreign car than death
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Selina moment - her priorities are completely backwards, more concerned with patriotic consumption than human life.

jokes.text: Actually, he was decapitated.
jokes.setup: Talk of spreading news like guts on pavement
jokes.punchline: Kent's matter-of-fact correction with worse detail
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kent - delivering horrible information with clinical precision.

jokes.text: Guess your age? Guess your weight?
jokes.setup: Selina at county fair
jokes.punchline: Carnival worker approaching president with insulting game
jokes.explanation: Simple cringe moment - the indignity of presidential campaigning.

jokes.text: You got any of those darts they use on rampaging circus animals?
jokes.setup: Nurse offering medication to help relax
jokes.punchline: Amy's request for animal tranquilizers
jokes.explanation: Great Amy line - comparing herself to dangerous circus animals while showing her anxiety about relaxing.

jokes.text: Is that the Berkeley VC-10? That's like the Shelby Cobra GT of vag-vacs.
jokes.setup: Dan seeing medical equipment
jokes.punchline: Car enthusiast knowledge applied to abortion equipment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dan moment - applying his car knowledge to medical equipment in the most inappropriate context.

jokes.text: What is up with the Clubfoot Cunt-tessa?
jokes.setup: Observing someone at the fair
jokes.punchline: Selina's cruel portmanteau nickname
jokes.explanation: Creative but cruel Selina nickname. Shows her wit but relies on disability mockery.

jokes.text: Yeah. Trying to screw me out of the presidency, then trying to actually screw me, then screwing me, then writing a book about screwing me. Also discussed it on television.
jokes.setup: Listing reasons not to trust Tom
jokes.punchline: Escalating list of betrayals, literal and metaphorical screwing
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure with wordplay on 'screwing.' Nice build through political to personal to media betrayal.

jokes.text: Are you deaf? Did you not hear what he was saying? What is wrong with you? Are you fuckin' retarded?!
jokes.setup: Jonah just finished apologizing for using slurs
jokes.punchline: Immediately uses the same slur again
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah irony - completely undoing his apology within seconds. Great structure and character consistency.

jokes.text: Mike McLintock has had enough!
jokes.setup: Mike struggling with various pressures
jokes.punchline: Third-person declaration of his limits
jokes.explanation: Good Mike moment - his breaking point expressed in characteristic awkward way.

jokes.text: you have to be more PC than a clit ring made out of wheatgrass.
jokes.setup: Warning Jonah to be politically correct
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific and absurd comparison involving hipster sexual imagery
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly weird Teddy line - combines sexual imagery with health food trends to create absurd PC comparison.

jokes.text: What the fuck? I gotta debate Dumbledong?
jokes.setup: Seeing opponent in wizard costume
jokes.punchline: Vulgar variation on Dumbledore
jokes.explanation: Typical Jonah crude wordplay. Character-appropriate but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: I'm sorry, you can't say that. I mean, only he can say that.
jokes.setup: Moderator says 'Niger' (the country)
jokes.punchline: Jonah thinks she said a slur and applies racial rules
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe comedy - Jonah's misunderstanding creates racist assumptions about legitimate country name.

jokes.text: Say what, 'Niger'? It doesn't matter how you pronounce it, we now know that it is wrong.
jokes.setup: Confusion over country name vs. slur
jokes.punchline: Jonah doubling down on his misunderstanding
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the misunderstanding, showing Jonah's complete inability to recognize his error.

jokes.text: I think that the United States should send troops into countries like N-word, and I think it would be best if those troops were black.
jokes.setup: Trying to answer foreign policy question
jokes.punchline: Offensive euphemism plus racist military policy
jokes.explanation: Shows Jonah's continued confusion but relies heavily on offensive content without much craft.

jokes.text: So we're talking about, what, $6?
jokes.setup: Learning Mike invested his life savings with Andrew
jokes.punchline: Selina's dismissive estimate of Mike's worth
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina cruelty - immediately dismissing Mike's financial loss with brutal efficiency.

jokes.text: Get that Benedict Ronald McDonald on the phone right now.
jokes.setup: Angry about Mike's betrayal
jokes.punchline: Creative combination of Benedict Arnold and Ronald McDonald
jokes.explanation: Creative Selina insult combining historical betrayal with clown imagery - shows her wit under pressure.

jokes.text: Dude, your mom's on the phone. She sounds pissed.
jokes.setup: Mike's phone ringing with angry Selina
jokes.punchline: Child's innocent interpretation of presidential call
jokes.explanation: Nice moment showing how Mike's family perceives his relationship with Selina.

jokes.text: is that she would like me to tell you that Senator Talbot here accidentally murdered her boyfriend in a reckless car accident when she was 16 years old. But you know what? I'm not gonna stoop to your level and say that, Selina.
jokes.setup: Tom claiming he won't stoop to negative attacks
jokes.punchline: Saying exactly what he claims he won't say
jokes.explanation: Brilliant political hypocrisy - Tom attacks while claiming moral high ground. Perfect structure.

jokes.text: My God! I have had it with this whimpering and whining from you and your generation! It's time for you to grow a pair and man the fuck up!
jokes.setup: Catherine trying to calm her mother
jokes.punchline: Selina's generational rant using masculine language
jokes.explanation: Sets up her debate moment perfectly - Selina's authentic frustration that becomes her winning line.

jokes.text: How about for once in your life you stop whining, you stop complaining, and just man up?
jokes.setup: Debate confrontation with Kemi about generational differences
jokes.punchline: Using Catherine's earlier advice as debate attack
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that shows Selina weaponizing her daughter's advice. Becomes her signature moment.

jokes.text: God bless America for hating woman almost as much as I do.
jokes.setup: Winning debate by attacking another woman
jokes.punchline: Selina's self-aware but unrepentant misogyny
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - Selina's complete self-awareness about her own internalized misogyny. Peak Veep.

jokes.text: Feels like... someone shoved a red-hot pair of barbecue tongs up my joy trail.
jokes.setup: Dan asking how Amy feels post-abortion
jokes.punchline: Amy's graphic but creative description of discomfort
jokes.explanation: Great Amy line - 'joy trail' is creative euphemism, barbecue tongs adds specific painful imagery.

jokes.text: I'm gonna need that back when you're done, 'cause Leon will pay big bucks for it.
jokes.setup: Sophie giving Amy frozen maxi-pads for comfort
jokes.punchline: Revealing ulterior motive involving sexual commerce
jokes.explanation: Dark revelation about Sophie's motivations that recontextualizes her apparent kindness.

jokes.text: How would you like the chance to help lead a divisive, physically repugnant underdog to historic victory?
jokes.setup: Teddy recruiting Amy for Jonah's campaign
jokes.punchline: Honest but insulting description of the candidate
jokes.explanation: Perfect Teddy - brutally honest about Jonah while still trying to make the job sound appealing.

jokes.text: I just found out from my stupid stepfather-- Father-in-law! From my stupid stepfather-in-law, that math was created by Muslims.
jokes.setup: Jonah sharing new 'knowledge'
jokes.punchline: Racist mathematical conspiracy theory with relationship confusion
jokes.explanation: Shows Jonah's gullibility and casual racism, plus his confusion about family relationships.

jokes.text: You gonna make me say it? We got race in the race.
jokes.setup: Discussion of endorsement challenges
jokes.punchline: Crude reduction of racial politics to rhyme
jokes.explanation: Blunt political observation with crude rhyme scheme - shows campaign's reductive thinking.
